licence: Real-ESRGAN (xinntao/Real-ESRGAN) is BSD-3-Clause. The realesr-general-x4v3 weights ship under the same repo; check the repo's terms before redistribution.

toolchain.note: Recorded so a future failure can be attributed. The host HBDK and the board HBRT must match; 3.7.0 host pairs with 4.0.5 board.

cosine.note: featuremap input with no_preprocess; the tile is fed as RGB CHW in [0,1]. Layer A is the per-output cosine hb_compile prints at the end of calibration.

task_metric.name: PSNR of the reconstructed HR vs true HR, with bicubic as the floor
task_metric.protocol: Take a real image as ground-truth HR, 4x INTER_AREA downscale to make the LR input, upscale, compare. A useful upscaler must beat bicubic. calib.py --golden saves a single isolated tile (in/out) for a board-vs-float layer-C comparison with no tile blending involved.
task_metric.measured_note: No PSNR/dB figure survives in the salvaged scripts. sr_ref.py implements the method (model vs bicubic vs ground-truth HR) but no recorded value was captured. Reconstruct with calib.py --golden + a board run; do not quote a number until measured.

measured.note: No latency, PSNR or cosine measurement survives in the salvaged scripts/configs for this build. The values below are null on purpose. Do not invent them; measure on an S100P with hrt_model_exec on a quiet board.
measured.hbm_mb_note: Not recorded precisely. The documented tile-AREA scaling puts the 128 tile at ~37 MB and the 256 tile at ~148 MB; treat those as the order-of-magnitude expectation, not a measured byte count for this rebuild.

builds.name: 128 tile (shipped, config.yaml)
builds.why: The on-board runtime already tiles the frame, so the small tile is the right one. Same per-pixel throughput as the larger tiles, a quarter of the instruction stream.

builds.name: 256 tile (config_256.yaml, not shipped)
builds.why: NOT more accurate. A compiled .hbm is mostly instruction stream and scales with input AREA: 256 is 4x the pixels, ~4x the size, for identical per-pixel quality. Only worth it if the runtime cannot tile.

board_verification.note: Not re-verified in this repo. The recipe reconstructs the method (export -> [0,1] tile calibration with a native/downscaled domain mix -> int8 compile) faithfully from the salvaged scripts; the three-layer close-out (A/B/C) and the PSNR task metric have not been re-run here. Verify before quoting numbers.
